condition: DiagnosticReport.code (LOINC) resolves to OMOP concept with domain_id = Observation
narrative_md: When a DiagnosticReport's LOINC code resolves to an OMOP concept with domain_id = Observation, the report produces one or more observation rows. This applies primarily to clinical document types -- history and physical notes, discharge summaries, evaluation and plan notes, consultation notes -- where the LOINC code represents a document class rather than a quantitative measurement or procedure. Each conclusionCode entry generates a separate row. This is the most frequently hit domain for DiagnosticReport resources in real-world EHR data (clinical notes are far more common than lab panel-level reports).

fields.notes: Surrogate key. Hash/sequence of DiagnosticReport.id + conclusionCode index. Must be unique across all observation sources.
Resolve Patient/{id} reference to integer person_id. Skip row if unresolvable.
LOINC code looked up in OMOP vocabulary. Must have domain_id = Observation. Use first LOINC coding found. 0 if concept not found.
Date component of effectiveDateTime, or effectivePeriod.start. Skip row if absent (ETL-German behavior).
Full timestamp from effectiveDateTime or effectivePeriod.start.
ETL-German maps category via SOURCE_VOCABULARY_ID_DIAGNOSTIC_REPORT_CATEGORY custom concept map. Common defaults: 32817 (EHR) or 32856 (Lab result).

sources.comment: Custom source_to_concept_map keyed on 'DiagnosticReport_Category'; category required — resource skipped if absent
Conceptual: category code maps to type concept

fields.notes: DiagnosticReport conclusions are coded, not numeric. Leave null.
ETL-German stores the SNOMED code string here (line 409). Recommended: use conclusionCode display text, or fall back to conclusion free text (truncated to 60 chars).

sources.comment: Stores the raw SNOMED code string (not display text) from conclusionCode at line 409

fields.notes: SNOMED conclusion code resolved to OMOP concept. ETL-German currently stores this in observation_source_concept_id instead. Recommended: store the resolved SNOMED concept here as the clinical value of the report.

sources.comment: Commented out at line 408 — ETL-German does not populate this field; SNOMED concept goes to observation_source_concept_id instead

fields.notes: ETL-German maps SNOMED composite expression interpretation attributes (from post-coordinated conclusionCode) to this field (line 425). Null if no interpretation is present.

sources.comment: Interpretation code from composite SNOMED expression (value after = in :{...}) resolved to OMOP concept at line 425; not set without composite expression

fields.notes: No units on DiagnosticReport-level observations. Leave null.
First performer reference resolved to provider_id. Also consider resultsInterpreter[0].
Resolve Encounter/{id} to visit_occurrence_id.
Leave null unless visit details are modeled.
Verbatim SNOMED code from conclusionCode. ETL-German stores this (line 405).
SNOMED conclusionCode resolved to OMOP concept ID. ETL-German stores the SNOMED concept here (line 404).

sources.comment: SNOMED conclusionCode base concept stored here (line 404); doubles as semantic value since value_as_concept_id is unused

fields.notes: No units. Leave null.
ETL-German stores the SNOMED interpretation concept code from composite SNOMED expressions (line 426). Null if no composite expression.
Verbatim SNOMED code. ETL-German does not explicitly set this field. Recommended: store the raw conclusionCode string.
Can link back to a related OMOP record if needed. Leave null.
Leave null.

entries.source_display: Hospital Admission history and physical note
entries.target_concept_name: Hospital Admission history and physical note

references.notes: Primary reference. createDiagnosticReportObservation() with domain routing, LOINC lookup, SNOMED conclusionCode, composite SNOMED splitting, qualifier from interpretation.
Skeleton only -- no observation-specific mapping file exists. Example resource uses LOINC 34117-2 (History and physical note) which resolves to Observation domain.
Parser only -- extracts code, status, effectivePeriod. No OMOP output mapping.

edge_cases.handling: ETL-German skips the resource entirely. Alternative: fall back to issued timestamp.

edge_cases.case: Status is registered, preliminary, cancelled, entered-in-error, or unknown
edge_cases.handling: Reject. Accept only final, amended, corrected, appended.
ETL-German skips the resource. Without a conclusion, there is no value to store. Alternative: create a row with null value fields to record the report's existence.

Each code produces a separate observation row. The observation_concept_id (report LOINC) is the same across all rows; only observation_source_concept_id, observation_source_value, and value_as_string differ.
ETL-German splits composite expressions: base code before : maps to observation_source_concept_id; the interpretation attribute after = maps to qualifier_concept_id and qualifier_source_value.
ETL-German splits on + and creates one row per component code.
ETL-German skips the resource. Alternative: default to observation_type_concept_id = 32817 (EHR).

edge_cases.case: LOINC code resolves to Measurement or Procedure domain
edge_cases.handling: Route to measurement or procedure_occurrence table instead.

edge_cases.case: Report with result[] but no code or conclusionCode
edge_cases.handling: No report-level row produced. The Observations in result[] are still mapped independently.
Not supported. OMOP requires a single person_id. Skip the resource.
ETL-German deletes existing OMOP rows by fhirLogicalId or fhirIdentifier before re-inserting.

edge_cases.case: conclusion text present but no conclusionCode
edge_cases.handling: ETL-German skips (requires conclusionCode). Alternative: store conclusion text in value_as_string and create a row with the LOINC report code. The conclusion text can also be mapped to the note table instead.
